title: Air Force Academy Training: Life and Cadence at AFA Dundigal
author: Flt Lt. S. Vardhan (Retd.)
data: Life at the Air Force Academy (AFA) Dundigal is a blend of intense physical training, academic excellence, and flying training. The day begins at 04:30 AM with physical conditioning, drills, or cross-country runs. This is followed by ground school lectures on aerodynamics, navigation, meteorology, and military law. Flight cadets undergo rigorous flight training on Pilatus and Kiran trainers, learning the discipline of cockpit drills, solo flying, and formation maneuvers, culminating in the passing out parade.
